title: Have quadlet generate environment params that inherit from host
body:
### Feature request description

`podman run --env MYENV ...` (note there is no value assigned)  will pass `MYENV` from the host to the container without having to set the value directly in the run command

I'd like to see quadlet support this feature too. As of `v5.5` defining a `.container` unit as

```ini
[Container]
Environment=MYENV
``` 

the generated `.service` unit will not have an `--env=MYENV` flag in `ExecStart`

### Suggest potential solution

The corresponding `.service` unit is generated such

```ini
[Servce]
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --env MYENV ...
```

### Have you considered any alternatives?

At the moment I'm having to `systemctl edit --full myapp.service` and manually add `--env MYENV` to the unit's `ExecStart`. I can't `EnvironmentHost=yes` because that brings in changes I don't want

### Additional context

https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.5.0/markdown/podman-run.1.html#:~:text=If%20an%20environment%20variable%20is%20specified%20without%20a%20value%2C%20Podman%20checks%20the%20host%20environment%20for%20a%20value%20and%20set%20the%20variable%20only%20if%20it%20is%20set%20on%20the%20host

comments.body: Arguably this is a bug for me. quadlet should not impose additional restrictions on top of the normal podman run --env syntax.

Thanks for reporting this. I can see that there's a double issue here causing this. 
The `Environment` key is handled here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/746cbf12c9251b66365a9acaced3445f11d2d4d1/pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go#L2207.
Then, `LookupAllKeyVal` looks for the `=` sign in the unit file and if it doesn't drops the key. But also `addKeys` adds the `=` sign into the command which is not good here because setting `FOO=` will define `FOO` with an empty value.

I suggest mimicking the behavior of Go's `encoding/json` package and its marshaling logic: if a pointer is nil and `omitempty` is set, the key is omitted from the JSON; if the pointer is non-nil, the value is included, whether empty or not. In the proposed fix, the environment variables key-value map will follow the same logic: if the value is nil, the variable is considered unset and should be propagated from the host; if it is set, it follows the original logic.
